title: Chief Chinedu Chukwuonye Declares Senate Ambition for Okigwe Zone 2027
excerpt: Respected industrialist and Chairman of Coduc Nigeria Limited officially declares intention to contest for Okigwe Zone Senate seat in the 2027 general elections.
body:
Chief Chinedu Chukwuonye, Chairman of Coduc Nigeria Limited and a respected industrialist from Isiala Mbano, has officially declared his intention to contest for the Senate seat representing Okigwe Zone in the 2027 general elections.

Speaking on his decision, Chief Chukwuonye said his ambition is rooted in a deep commitment to restoring dignity, development, and opportunity to the people of Okigwe Zone.

"For too long, our people have endured poor roads, lack of electricity, unemployment, insecurity, and limited access to basic services. This is not the standard we deserve. It is time to move from neglect to development," he stated.

Chief Chukwuonye brings with him decades of private sector experience as a builder and infrastructure developer. As Chairman of Coduc Nigeria Limited, he has led projects across construction, manufacturing, and community development, creating jobs and supporting economic growth.

His campaign will focus on infrastructure development, reliable electricity, youth employment, community security, healthcare access, and education. He called on youths, community leaders, women, and all stakeholders across Okigwe Zone to join him in building a future anchored on opportunity, accountability, and shared progress.

title: CCM Launches Youth Skills Empowerment Drive Across Isiala Mbano
excerpt: The Chief Chukwuonye Movement rolls out a skills acquisition programme targeting 200 young people across Isiala Mbano communities.
body:
As part of his commitment to grassroots development ahead of the 2027 Senate race, Chief Chinedu Chukwuonye has initiated a structured youth empowerment programme targeting 200 young men and women across Isiala Mbano.

The programme, which covers vocational training in welding, electrical installation, fashion design, and digital skills, is fully funded by Chief Chukwuonye through his community development fund.

"Jobs do not come from promises. They come from equipping people with skills and creating the environment for them to succeed," Chief Chukwuonye said at the opening ceremony.

Beneficiaries receive training materials, mentorship, and a start-up kit upon completion. The programme is expected to expand to other LGAs within Okigwe Zone before the end of the year.

title: Community Leaders and Traditional Rulers Back Chief Chukwuonye's Senate Bid
excerpt: Traditional rulers and community leaders from across Okigwe Zone formally express support for Chief Chinedu Chukwuonye's 2027 Senate campaign.
body:
In a show of broad community confidence, a delegation of traditional rulers, women leaders, and community development union presidents from all three LGAs in Okigwe Zone met with Chief Chinedu Chukwuonye to formally endorse his Senate candidacy.

The delegation, led by a prominent traditional ruler from Okigwe LGA, cited Chief Chukwuonye's track record of community investment, his private sector credibility, and his deep roots in the zone as key reasons for their support.

"We have watched him build. We have seen him deliver. Okigwe needs a Senator who has already proven he can get things done before he is even elected," the delegation's statement read.

Chief Chukwuonye thanked the leaders and reiterated his commitment to ensuring every community in Okigwe Zone feels represented, heard, and served.

title: CCM Advocates for Okigwe Road Restoration — Promises Senate Bill Within 90 Days
excerpt: Chief Chukwuonye commits to introducing the Okigwe Road Restoration Bill within 90 days of being sworn into the Senate.
body:
Chief Chinedu Chukwuonye has made a firm commitment to table the Okigwe Road Restoration Bill in the Senate within 90 days of his swearing-in, making it one of the most specific and auditable pledges made by any candidate in the zone.

The bill, which has been developed in consultation with engineers, community leaders, and local government officials, targets the rehabilitation of the Okigwe–Enugu corridor, five major market access roads, and rural feeder roads serving farming communities.

"Roads are not a luxury. Roads are how a mother gets her children to school. Roads are how a farmer gets to the market. Roads are how a zone tells the world it matters. Okigwe has been told it does not matter for too long," Chief Chukwuonye stated.

He challenged all other candidates in the zone to make equally specific, nameable, and trackable commitments.

title: Chief Chukwuonye Hosts Women of Okigwe Forum — Pledges Healthcare and Water Access
excerpt: Hundreds of women from across Okigwe Zone gather for CCM's first community forum focused entirely on women's issues.
body:
Hundreds of women from markets, schools, churches, and community associations across Okigwe Zone attended the first CCM Women's Forum hosted by Chief Chinedu Chukwuonye in Okigwe town.

The forum focused on the issues women in the zone identified as most urgent: access to clean water, maternal healthcare, market road conditions, and security.

Chief Chukwuonye announced a pre-election commitment to commission boreholes in ten underserved wards and to advocate for a mobile health unit programme serving communities more than 15 kilometres from a health facility.

"When a mother cannot access clean water, the whole family suffers. When a pregnant woman cannot reach a clinic safely, it is a failure of leadership, not a failure of medicine. I will not accept that as normal for Okigwe," he told the gathering.

The forum concluded with women's representatives formally aligning with the CCM movement.

title: CCM Volunteer Network Launches — Thousands Sign Up Across Okigwe Zone
excerpt: The Chief Chukwuonye Movement formally launches its volunteer network, with sign-ups across all wards in Okigwe Zone.
body:
The Chief Chukwuonye Movement (CCM) has formally launched its volunteer network, with thousands of supporters across all wards in Okigwe Zone signing up to participate in the 2027 campaign.

Volunteers have been organised into ward-level teams responsible for community outreach, event attendance, content distribution, and ground-level intelligence gathering.

CCM Campaign Coordinator stated: "This movement is not built on money. It is built on people. Every person who signs up is telling Okigwe that they are ready to take responsibility for their own future."

Chief Chukwuonye addressed the volunteers via a video message, urging them to be ambassadors of the movement's core message: from neglect to development.

"You are not just volunteers. You are the movement. Go into your communities and tell the truth about what we deserve and what we are going to build together."
